name: Stir-Fried Pork Tenderloin with Vegetables
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pork tenderloin crosswise into 1/8"-thick slices using a knife held in a slanting position, almost parallel to the cutting surface.
2. In a medium-sized bowl, mix the pork with soy sauce, sherry, cornstarch, sugar, gingerroot, and salad oil.
3. In a 12-inch skillet or wok over high heat, add tablespoons of hot salad oil. Cook the broccoli, mushrooms, carrot, and salt, stirring quickly and frequently (stir frying) until the vegetables are coated with oil.
4. Add water and continue stir-frying until the vegetables are tender-crisp. Spoon onto a warm platter.
5. In the same skillet over high heat, add 2 more tablespoons of hot salad oil. Cook the pork mixture until the pork loses its pink color, about 2-3 minutes, stirring quickly and frequently.
6. Return the cooked vegetables to the skillet with the pork and heat through.
